title: rosa communis var. ostensa
intro: Rosa communis var. ostensa, also known as wild rose or dog rose, is a plant species belonging to the genus Rosa, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, wounds, and fever. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. ostensa include its ability to reduce stress, promote sleep, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, while also being rich in vitamins and minerals. Rosa communis var. ostensa can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its hips (fruits) can be consumed raw or dried to make rosehip tea, a popular remedy for colds and flu.
